title: pkcs11 put create-before-destroy ordering: do not revert
description: The create-before-destroy ordering in pkcs11 put is the only correct choice given PKCS#11 has no atomic replace. Failure before create_object leaves existing secret intact. Failure after create_object leaves a duplicate that next put cleans up. Data loss is not possible. A simplify suggestion to use destroy-then-create would introduce a write-window data loss if create fails after destroy. The comment block documents this exactly.
